title: Add an OPT_GLOBAL to apply to the run-time library
body:
As I mentioned in #2365, I think there is a use-case for OPT_FAST=-O0 but still compiling the run-time library with higher optimization as it can significantly improve tracing speed which you want during a debug cycle. I am concios about adding more complexity to the build so I will do some measurements on this to confirm this indeed makes sense. If it is, adding an OPT_GLOBAL or similar to apply to the run-time library is probably in order. I would think this should default with `OPT_GLOBAL = $(OPT_FAST)`, but overrideable by the user. Let me know if you have anything against this. In the end this can always be achieved with external makery.

If you think we should do this it would be good to get it into the next release in order to break people's makefiles only once (which is not what I am trying to do)!
